prose: **Ferrocene Fukui Reactivity Analysis** — GFN2-xTB (xtb) semi-empirical tight-binding method, gas phase, charge 0 (neutral parent), multiplicity 1. The calculation computed Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) by calculating Mulliken partial charges for the neutral molecule, cation (charge +1, multiplicity 2), and anion (charge −1, multiplicity 2). SCF converged for all three states; charge conservation checks passed (Σ(f⁺) = 1.0000, Σ(f⁻) = 1.0000). The most reactive site is the iron center: f⁺ (electrophilic) = 0.22885456, f⁻ (nucleophilic) = 0.25938191. The dual descriptor (f⁻ − f⁺) at Fe is −0.030527354, indicating slightly higher nucleophilic susceptibility. Cyclopentadienyl hydrogens show secondary reactivity (f⁺ ≈ 0.0635, f⁻ ≈ 0.0619 each). Geometry: input structure used as-is (single-point, not optimized). Command: `chemkit fukui --method xtb --charge 0 --mult 1 ferrocene.xyz`. Output file: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp9kilwtqc/live.json`. Reactivity plot PNG: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/argo_claude-haiku-4.5/20260704-181706_ferrocene_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`. No warnings reported. Integrity status: trustworthy.
